entry.title: Configuring Azure for Machine Learning with Keras and Tensorflow
entry.summary:
Due to recent hype I have decided to look into machine learning and its uses. I have a decent computer powered by a Nvidia 980ti, which is more than capable of machine learning. This is great for most tasks, but as I get a bunch of Azure credits each month with my MSDN licence, I figured I would make the most of Microsoft’s new GPU servers.

This post is aimed at getting an environment up and running that works with Tensorflow and Keras. I decided to use Python 3 over 2.

entry.title: A brief overview of VR
entry.summary: In this post I hope to explain, without using too much jargon, what Virtual Reality (VR) is. There are a lot of exciting things happening in the area of VR right now, so information gets outdated fast. The basic information in this post will largely remain the same, but some of the vendor and technology specific parts will be updated over time.

entry.title: Glossary of VR terms
entry.summary: I have found there to be quite a lot of jargon surrounding VR, so I’ve to put together a rough list of what some of the words actually mean. This is by no means a comprehensive list, and it only focuses on terms in the context of VR. The list also does not contain some of the more controversial words like “metaverse”, where there seem to be arguments over what it even is.

entry.title: Configuring Node & NPM to work within a corperate Windows environment
entry.summary: For those of you who need to use NPM at work, you may have found that some things don’t work out of the box. For example, your work’s corporate proxy to cause a few issues. I also had some issues with particular packages not working in the default install location.

entry.title: Obligatory First Post
entry.summary: So, I started writing a blog. This means that I need to write one of those obligatory first blog posts where I tell you about me and why
